Commercial Mar 23, 2026

7 New Nonstop Routes In Just 5 Days: Where Alaska Airlines & United Airlines Fly Now

One of them have never been served by any airline before. Find out more!

Route expansion in underserved markets signals carriers are betting on post-pandemic demand recovery and competitive positioning in regional connectivity.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 23, 2026

De Havilland Canada Delivers 1,000th Twin Otter

Milestone delivery adds another chapter to a utility aircraft whose history dates to 1965.

The Twin Otter's 60-year production run demonstrates unprecedented market demand for rugged, versatile utility aircraft in remote operations, suggesting sustained profitability in niche aviation segments.

See AVweb →

Safety Mar 23, 2026

Is LaGuardia More Dangerous Than Other US Airports?

LaGuardia’s hazards are real, but its reputation may be bigger than its actual danger.

LaGuardia's accident risk profile may not significantly exceed peer major airports despite its challenging geography and infrastructure constraints.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 23, 2026

NASA to Provide Update on Implementation of National Space Policy

NASA will host a public event at 9 a.m. EDT on Tuesday, March 24, at the Mary W. Jackson NASA Headquarters in Washington to outline how the agency is executing President Donald J. Trump’s National Space Policy and accelerating preparations for America’s return to the surface of the Moon by 2028. ...

NASA's accelerated Moon return timeline of 2028 under Trump's space policy signals increased resource allocation and operational urgency for lunar infrastructure and human spaceflight programs.

See NASA →

How to Assess Safety Risk

A practical walkthrough of safety risk assessment under 14 CFR Part 5.

Part 5's structured risk assessment framework provides operators with standardized methods to identify hazards before they escalate into safety events.

Aerospace Mar 23, 2026

NASA’s Hubble Revisits Crab Nebula to Track 25 Years of Expansion

A quarter-century after its first observations of the full Crab Nebula, NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope has taken a fresh look at the supernova remnant. The result is an unparalleled, detailed look at the aftermath of a supernova and how it has evolved over Hubble’s long lifetime. A paper detailing...

Hubble's quarter-century monitoring of Crab Nebula expansion provides critical validation data for supernova physics models that inform aerospace propulsion and energy generation research.

See NASA →

Commercial Mar 23, 2026

This Airline Has The World’s Best Economy Class Seat In 2026

Japan Airlines was recently given the award for the best economy class seat in the world.

JAL's economy seat advancement signals a competitive shift where carriers are investing in premium coach experiences to differentiate on long-haul routes and improve revenue per available seat mile.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Mar 23, 2026

Air Canada Jet Crash at LaGuardia Kills Two Pilots

Crash shuts down major New York airport, injures dozens and disrupts hundreds of flights.

Airport closure from fatal crash will cascade operational disruptions across Northeast aviation networks for days, straining recovery capacity.

Safety Mar 23, 2026

Safety Culture in Aviation SMS

How organizational culture shapes safety outcomes and what it takes to build a reporting culture that supports SMS.

Strong reporting cultures reduce accidents by enabling proactive hazard identification before incidents occur, making SMS effectiveness dependent on leadership commitment to psychological safety.

Safety Mar 23, 2026

Squawk 7700: Aer Lingus A330 Briefly Becomes World's Most Tracked Plane Amid Medical Emergency

The plane landed safely in the Irish capital, where it was met by emergency services.

Medical emergencies triggering emergency broadcasts create real-time crowd-sourced flight tracking spikes that highlight aviation's transparency and global interest in safety outcomes.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 23, 2026

Here’s Why The US Navy Still Needs The F/A-18 Super Hornet In 2026

With over 4 million flight hours and continuous upgrades, the combat-proven Super Hornet remains a cornerstone of carrier air wings.

The Navy's reliance on the aging F/A-18 Super Hornet through 2026 reveals critical delays in next-generation fighter development and sustained demand for reliable legacy platforms in operational readiness.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 23, 2026

Press Release: Finnair orders up to 46 Embraer E195-E2 twinjets

Embraer and Finnair have signed an agreement for up to 46 E195-E2 aircraft, comprising 18 firm orders, 16 options, and 12 purchase rights. The E195-E2 will replace Finnair’s older aircraft, supporting the airline’s strategy for profitable growth. Selected for its superior efficiency, reliability,...

Finnair's commitment to 46 E195-E2 aircraft signals confidence in regional twin-jet economics for profitable European operations while modernizing its fleet.

See Runway Girl Network →

Safety Mar 23, 2026

FAA Aviation Safety at Oshkosh

Go behind the scenes at EAA AirVenture with the FAA's Aviation Safety organization. Meet the people who help ensure safety at this event and provide essential information resources to the general aviation community.

FAA's on-site safety personnel at AirVenture actively engage general aviation pilots with real-time compliance guidance and risk mitigation strategies beyond standard regulatory enforcement.

Commercial Mar 23, 2026

Massive Boost: Singapore Airlines Adds Airbus A380 Flights On This Critical Long-Haul Route

Superjumbo service returns to this hugely important route after a three-year absence.

Singapore Airlines' A380 redeployment signals confidence in ultra-long-haul premium cabin demand recovery and efficient deployment of high-capacity aircraft on profitable routes.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 23, 2026

10 longest US Domestic

Until the latest schedule update, United Airlines was to operate the US's longest domestic route in 2026. It was to be from Newark to Honolulu. But it has now been relegated to the second spot. But what are the top 10 longest domestic US flights this year?

Alaska Airlines' Seattle-Honolulu expansion has reclaimed the longest domestic route title from United's planned Newark service, signaling intensifying competition for ultra-long domestic markets.

See Simple Flying →

Drones Mar 23, 2026

DJI sues Insta360 days before Avata 360 drone launch

Just as DJI is gearing up to unveil its first-ever 360-degree FPV drone, the Avata 360, the company has launched a legal offensive against its fastest-rising rival, Insta360. And this time, the battle isn’t about specs or features — it’s about who owns the underlying technology powering the next ...

DJI's pre-launch lawsuit against Insta360 signals intensifying IP battles as 360-degree FPV drone technology becomes a critical competitive differentiator in the consumer drone market.

See DroneDJ →

Industry Mar 23, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across airlines, airports, and flight planning can simultaneously address capacity constraints and environmental concerns without waiting for new aircraft technology.

See MDPI →

Military Mar 23, 2026

B-52s are now Launching from RAF Fairford Loaded with JDAM Bombs

After initially employing only AGM-158 JASSM stand-off weapons, B-52s are now launching from RAF Fairford with the external pylons fully loaded with GBU-31 JDAM bombs. After weeks flying only with AGM-158 Joint Air-to-Surface Standoff Missiles (JASSM) under their wings, the U.S. Air Force B-52H S...

B-52 deployments from RAF Fairford now include precision-guided munitions on external pylons, expanding operational payload flexibility beyond standoff missiles.

See The Aviationist →

beOnd to launch flights to Paris and London, add frequency to Zurich, Munich

Luxury airline beOnd announced on March 23, 2026, that it will be connecting Male (MLE), in the Maldives,… The post beOnd to launch flights to Paris and London, add frequency to Zurich, Munich appeared first on AeroTime.

beOnd's expansion into major European leisure and business hubs signals growing demand for ultra-premium point-to-point services beyond traditional long-haul markets.

Russian Progress cargo craft to dock manually with ISS after antenna issue

A Russian Progress cargo spacecraft heading to the International Space Station (ISS) will have to be docked manually… The post Russian Progress cargo craft to dock manually with ISS after antenna issue appeared first on AeroTime.

Manual docking procedures remain critical contingency capability for ISS resupply operations when automated systems fail, highlighting dependence on ground control expertise.

2 Extra Inches On 237 Aircraft: easyJet Orders New Airbus A320neo Seats As Newcastle Base Opens

The British low-cost carrier is investing heavily in its fleet and network.

easyJet's seat density optimization demonstrates how LCCs extract incremental revenue gains through cabin reconfiguration rather than aircraft expansion.

Commercial Mar 23, 2026

6-Hour Lines: Delta Staff Step In As TSA Shortages Paralyze Atlanta Hub

Errors on the airport's website have compounded the situation.

Delta's operational resilience in covering TSA gaps reveals how airlines are becoming de facto security partners when government staffing fails, creating liability and service quality risks.

Commercial Mar 23, 2026

A Look At The Salaries Of Commercial Widebody Captains At Major US Airlines

For many, becoming a widebody captain is a the final stage of a pilot's aviation career and when they earn the highest salary.

Major US carriers compensate widebody captains at $300k-$400k annually, reflecting two decades of seniority investments and the airline's operational risk exposure on international routes.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 23, 2026

SNC’s AEGIR-W Sea Drone Washes Ashore on Turkey’s Black Sea Coast

The AEGIR-W Unmanned Surface Vessel mysteriously ran aground on the Black Sea Coast in northeastern Turkey and was later blown up by Turkish authorities. An Unmanned Surface Vessel (USV) was found washed ashore on northeastern Turkey’s Black Sea coast in the Ordu region early in the afternoon of ...

Loss of autonomous maritime platform highlights operational risks and geopolitical sensitivities when unmanned systems operate near contested strategic waterways without reliable recovery protocols.

See The Aviationist →

There’s a reason scientists are increasingly turning to drones to understand climate change, and a new mission in Greenland shows exactly why. more…

Drone-based environmental monitoring is becoming critical infrastructure for climate research, expanding commercial drone applications beyond traditional inspection and mapping into scientific discovery.

Finnair's dual aircraft strategy signals a strategic shift toward regional capacity expansion while modernizing its narrowbody fleet for competitive positioning in Nordic markets.

Two dead after Air Canada Express CRJ900 hits fire truck at LaGuardia

Regulatory Mar 22, 2026

EASA publishes the updated Easy Access Rules for Large Rotorcraft (CS-29)

EASA publishes the updated Easy Access Rules for Large Rotorcraft (CS-29) European Union Aviation Safety Agency has published the Amendment 12 of the Easy Access Rules for Large Rotorcraft (CS-29) available in PDF, machine-readable XML, and in a new improved online version with: Annalisa Farina M...

Amendment 12 introduces machine-readable XML and an improved online format to the CS-29 rules, significantly enhancing accessibility and integration capabilities for rotorcraft manufacturers and operators.

See EASA →

Military Mar 22, 2026

The Tomcat’s Six Pack: The F-14 Six on Six Phoenix Test

Designed for fleet air defense, the F-14 was armed with a potent long-range missile and, with a powerful new radar, could engage multiple airborne targets at once. The United States Navy’s F-14 Tomcat was the most potent fleet defense aircraft of its time. Its AIM-54 Phoenix missile had a long ra...

The F-14's capability to simultaneously engage six targets with AIM-54 Phoenix missiles established a revolutionary multi-target engagement standard that influenced naval air defense doctrine for decades.

See The Aviationist →

Military Mar 22, 2026

U.S. Army Receives DARPA’s Optionally Piloted H-60Mx Black Hawk

The U.S. Army will put the H-60Mx and Sikorsky’s MATRIX flight autonomy software through a rigorous operational testing campaign with real world logistics in contested scenarios. The U.S. Army received on Mar. 19, 2026, the H-60Mx Black Hawk Optionally Piloted Vehicle (OPV) at Fort Eustis, Virgin...

Army's autonomous Black Hawk testing in contested logistics scenarios validates DARPA's MATRIX autonomy for real-world military operations beyond lab environments.

See The Aviationist →

Commercial Mar 22, 2026

How Quvia helps airlines throw out the IFC playbook and take control

The inflight connectivity marketplace is moving very quickly. And aviation industry stakeholders have never seen anything like it before. The cycles are getting faster. The technology is evolving rapidly, including the antenna hardware. At least five or more broadband Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satell...

Airlines can now bypass traditional IFC vendors and directly manage connectivity solutions through marketplace platforms, reducing dependency on legacy providers and accelerating technology adoption cycles.

Military Mar 22, 2026

Video Shows Saab 340 Airborne Radar Flying in Ukraine

The Saab 340 AEW, also called the S 100D Argus, will enhance Ukraine’s situational awareness when facing Russian cruise missile and kamikaze drone barrages. Ukraine appears to have received its first Airborne Early Warning and Control (AEW&C) aircraft, after the Swedish Saab 340 airborne radar wa...

Ukraine's acquisition of airborne early warning capability significantly elevates its air defense coordination against distributed Russian missile and drone attacks.

See The Aviationist →
